text:
Notable groups include the Indigenous Peoples Ancestral Knowledge Working Group, the Economics Working Group, and the Legislative Working Group (led by CELDF) [cite: 1, 2].
**Community size:**
The alliance has grown to over 1,500 individual members and more than 140 member organizations representing every continent [cite: 2].
---

## Metagov

**Name:** Metagov
**Type:** Research Collective / Non-Profit
**Orientation:** SPACESHIP
**Website:** https://metagov.org
**Location:** USA / Global
**Scale:** Medium
**Maturity:** Maturing
**Flags:** Direct Collaborator Potential (Very High)

**What they do:**
Metagov is a research collective that builds interoperable governance tools and studies digital and physical governance systems [cite: 6, 7].
They focus on making collective intelligence tools more open, modular, and capture-resistant [cite: 8].
Metagov acts as a "hub-shaped" organization, providing administrative, financial, and digital infrastructure for independent researchers to spin up democracies, juries, and online economies [cite: 6, 9, 10].
They operate robust grant programs and working groups to build flat-file interoperability standards for deliberative tech [cite: 7, 8].
**Key people:**
*   Liz Barry, Executive Director and Research Director (RD) [cite: 6, 7]
*   Joshua Tan, RD and Founding Executive Director [cite: 6, 11]
*   Nathan Schneider, RD and Board President [cite: 11]
*   Aviv Ovadya, RD (Interoperable Deliberative Tools project) [cite: 7, 8]
*   Evan Miyazono, RD [cite: 11]
*   Amy Zhang, RD [cite: 9, 10]
*   Eugene Leventhal, RD [cite: 10, 11]
*   Seth Frey, RD [cite: 6, 11]
*   Philipp Zahn, RD [cite: 11]
*   Daniel Carr, Senior Research Engineer [cite: 11]
*   Miriam Ashton, Lead Research Engineer [cite: 9]
*   Lee DeSota, Lead Facilitator and Project Manager [cite: 8, 10]
*   Cecile Green, Lead for Open Facilitation Library [cite: 8]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Mina Foundation [cite: 8]
*   AI & Democracy Foundation [cite: 11]
*   Atlas Computing [cite: 11]
*   Society Library [cite: 7]
*   Open Collective [cite: 9, 12]
*   DAOstar / OpenGrants [cite: 13]
*   DADA.art (Launch partner) [cite: 9]
*   Loomio, SourceCred, Stanford Participatory Budgeting Platform [cite: 9]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Interoperable Deliberative Tools Symposia and in-person offsites (Berkeley, Oct 2024; Jan 2025) [cite: 7, 8, 10]
*   Mathematics for Governance Design workshop (University of Edinburgh) [cite: 11]
*   Dweb Camp (AI Think Tank curation) [cite: 11]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools:** Metagov Core API, PolicyKit, CommunityRule, Govbase, OpenGrants data layer, Open Facilitation Library [cite: 8, 9, 13].
*   **Publications:** *Governable Spaces* by Nathan Schneider; DAOIP-5 standard for Web3 grant data; Interop Mid-term reports [cite: 10, 11, 13].
**Funding sources:**
*   Mina Foundation ($200,000 grant for the Interoperable Deliberative Tools program) [cite: 8, 10]
*   Stanford's Digital Civil Society Lab [cite: 9]
*   Ford Foundation and Sloan Foundation (via Open Collective) [cite: 12]
*   Grant for the Web (GFTW) [cite: 9]

**Key projects:**
*   Interoperable Deliberative Tools (IDT or "Interop") [cite: 7, 8]
*   Public AI [cite: 11]
*   Web 3 and DAOs [cite: 11]
*   Calm Tech Institute [cite: 11]
*   OpenGrants by DAOstar (creating a composable grants data layer for Web3) [cite: 13]

**Collaboration potential:**
Very High.
Metagov is directly building the backend software required to "spin up democracies, governance boards, reputation systems, [and] juries" [cite: 9].
Their infrastructure is custom-built for governance simulations.
**Governance model:**
Operates a decentralized "hub-shaped" model.
Eighteen Research Directors act as principal investigators who leverage Metagov as a fiscal host while retaining modular independence for their working groups [cite: 6, 10].
**Community size:**
18 active Research Directors; the Interop cohort alone consists of 21 developer teams [cite: 6, 7, 8].
---

## Not Only Larp

**Name:** Not Only Larp
**Type:** Company / Design Studio
**Orientation:** ELEUSINIAN MYSTERIES (Temple)
**Website:** https://notonlylarp.com
**Location:** Italy / Spain
**Scale:** Medium
**Maturity:** Mature
**Flags:** Direct Collaborator Potential (High)

**What they do:**
Not Only Larp is a European non-profit design studio that creates immersive Live Action Role-Play (LARP) experiences explicitly aimed at raising awareness and inspiring social change, a practice they term "larptivism" [cite: 14].
They design large-scale, emotionally intense environments where players explore profound moral, ethical, and societal dilemmas, ranging from dystopian revolutions to the implications of artificial consciousness [cite: 14, 15, 16].
**Key people:**
*   Esperanza (Espe) Montero, Producer, Project Manager, and Creative Lead [cite: 16, 17]
*   Linden Lewis, Art Director, NPC GM, and Writer [cite: 17]
*   Enrique Esturillo, Safety Designer, GM, and Writer [cite: 17]
*   Alexandra 'Lu' Beck, Plot Lead, Designer, and GM [cite: 17]
*   Sergio Durán [cite: 17]
*   Kai Simon Fredriksen (In memoriam) [cite: 17]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Ultimacy [cite: 17]
*   Asociación Fénix [cite: 17]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Host multiple ongoing LARPs across Europe (primarily Spain and Italy) [cite: 14]
*   Para Jugar (A Spanish LARP convention for chamber LARPs) [cite: 14]

**Key projects:**
*   *Conscience*: A sci-fi LARP inspired by Westworld exploring artificial consciousness and humanity's appetites [cite: 14, 15, 18].
